title: rehydrate manifest
description: Machine-readable snapshot manifest emitted by the backup process and consumed by the restore process.

schema_version.description: Semver version of this schema. Additive changes bump the minor version; breaking changes bump the major version.

created_at.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp at which this snapshot was created.

snapshot_id.description: Unique identifier for this snapshot, typically '<hostname>-<created_at>' with colons stripped.

source_machine.description: Properties of the machine that produced this snapshot.

user.description: Unix username that ran the backup.

path.description: Ordered PATH entries at backup time.

parent_snapshot.description: snapshot_id of the immediately preceding snapshot in an incremental chain. Null for full snapshots (all v0.1 snapshots are full).

categories.description: Map of category name to category payload. Known categories are typed via $defs; unknown categories are permitted to allow forward compatibility.

^[a-z][a-z0-9_-]*$.description: Unknown future category. Must be an object with at minimum a 'strategy' discriminator.

strategy.description: Discriminator identifying how the restore process should interpret this category's payload.

dotfiles_category.description: Dotfiles category payload: a flat list of files captured from $HOME.

strategy.description: Discriminator value for the dotfiles category.

files.description: Ordered list of captured dotfile entries.

file_entry.description: A single captured file or symlink.

path.description: File path relative to $HOME. Must not start with '/'.

object_hash.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the file's contents (64 lowercase hex characters). For symlinks this is the hash of the link target string.

mode.description: POSIX file mode as a 4-digit octal string.

mtime.description: File modification time as ISO 8601 UTC.

size.description: File size in bytes. 0 for symlinks (the content is the target string, not the resolved file).

is_symlink.description: True if the entry is a symbolic link rather than a regular file.

symlink_target.description: Symlink target path (may be relative or absolute). Null when is_symlink is false.
